small fist-size greenish apples growing over concrete fence on the East side of Pioneer Lane.  Sweet and tasty on September 22! Access: Private but overhanging. Falling Fruit season: September-October.
Falling Fruit access: Private but overhanging.
tiny plums, mostly purple.  Slightly sour, but edible.  Need another frosty week and they'll be great! Access: Private but overhanging. Falling Fruit season: September-October.
Falling Fruit access: Private but overhanging.

These are tiny wild strawberries about the size of your little fingernail if you're lucky.  They grow along the forest service trails that switchback up to the main Hagen trail.  They don't fruit every season, or they do and the critters get to them first.  Yo

Apple tree with decent size apples (slightly smaller than your fist).  It's located on an undeveloped lot.  I'm sure it's private, but I've seen multiple families out harvesting bags of fruit in season... I wouldn't know who to ask for permission. Access: Priv

Raspberries grow through the fence at Mike Yokel Park.  Ready to pick in late July and August, but you've got to get to the park early in the day if you want to beat the kids to the fruit;) Access: Private but overhanging. Falling Fruit season: July-August.
Falling Fruit access: Private but overhanging.

These raspberries are on the East side of the yoga studio... they are abundant and never harvested by anyone!  They do belong to the yoga studio, and when I asked, they were surprised to hear that they were out there (?) and said go ahead.  I would re-ask each
There are huge crab apples hanging off medium size trees. At the churches door, so ask Pastor or front desk... They will most likely say yes. Access: Private. Falling Fruit season: July-September.
